title: gh-148105: _pyrepl: switch console refresh to structured rendered screens
author: pablogsal

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body: Crap I activated this copilot nonsense by mistake ...

comments.author: chris-eibl
comments.body:
Great work - Thanks!

Wasn't able to do a detailed review, yet, but played a little bit with it.

When pasting on Windows, the lines are postfixed with `^M` for me.
Example:
```
class foo:
    pass
```

Pasting result using Windows terminal
```
>>> class foo:^M
...     pass^M
...
```

Happens also using the legacy console in paste mode.

comments.author: johnslavik
comments.body: I'll check the Windows issue. Filed https://github.com/pablogsal/cpython/pull/123 for more docs, lmk if you'd like this!

comments.author: johnslavik
comments.body:
On Windows I found a problem with history paging exit:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5d0960dc-c2ff-4f30-9f64-063aa908df88

~~Clearing seems to be a fix on Windows, not sure it makes sense on Unix yet.~~ NVM, this breaks Unix.

<details>

<summary>Patch</summary>

```
diff --git a/Lib/_pyrepl/commands.py b/Lib/_pyrepl/commands.py
index 90bafa288c0..744a1be71f6 100644
--- a/Lib/_pyrepl/commands.py
+++ b/Lib/_pyrepl/commands.py
@@ -493,9 +493,10 @@ def do(self) -> None:
 
         # We need to copy over the state so that it's consistent between
         # console and reader, and console does not overwrite/append stuff
+        self.reader.invalidate_prompt()
         trace("command.show_history sync_rendered_screen")
         self.reader.console.sync_rendered_screen(
-            self.reader.rendered_screen,
+            RenderedScreen.empty(),
             self.reader.cxy,
         )
```

</details>

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body:
i pt a short commit with fixes. I think this should fix both Windows issues:

* Paste `^M`: `ch += "\n"` → `ch = "\n"` to replace `\r` instead of concatenating. Thanks @johnslavik and @chris-eibl.

* I replaced `sync_rendered_screen(old_state)` with `console.clear()` + `invalidate_full()`. Uses `clear()` instead of syncing with empty screen to avoid the `grow_lines` issue on Unix that @johnslavik found.


Con you pls check this fixed the issue?

comments.author: johnslavik
comments.body:
Great! CRLF fixed.

<details>

<summary>To fix later</summary>

Do we want to make sure leading lines stay rendered in history search mode?
Same for paste mode

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/636d94f1-3438-4261-949d-64eb0ef0f976

I think in `get_prompt` we'd have to examine `ps1` again for leading lines?
Hmm, maybe this can be fixed later, I think it might be out of scope

</details>

comments.author: johnslavik
comments.body:
> * I replaced `sync_rendered_screen(old_state)` with `console.clear()` + `invalidate_full()`. Uses `clear()` instead of syncing with empty screen to avoid the `grow_lines` issue on Unix that @johnslavik found.

I tested it on Windows and ran into some other, new issues (discussed privately), I gave it a shot and history mode exiting should be fully fixed by https://github.com/pablogsal/cpython/pull/124.

comments.author: chris-eibl
comments.body:
> Great! CRLF fixed.

Yupp, works for me, too 👍

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body: Thanks a lot everyone for the help and the reviews. You rock 🤘

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body:
Hi @pablogsal , in this PR, the old logic of `_pyrepl.util.disp_str` has changed so `\n` is not kept as it is anymore. It is now converted to `^J`. It might be an implementation detail of `_pyrepl`, but `pdb` is using this method to display code. Now the code looks like

```
> /home/gaogaotiantian/programs/mycpython/example.py(1)<module>()
-> x = 1
(Pdb) ll
  1  -> x = 1^J
  2     breakpoint()^J
(Pdb) 
```

Is it intentional that `disp_str` now displays `\n` as `^J`? If so, what mitigation I should use on pdb side to make it work? I can of course strip the trailing `\n` before passing it to `disp_str`, that's okay if that's the easiest way to make it work. Just wondering if there are more official ways to fix it.
